goal: Prepare a brief on recent changes in eating habits
constraints: Content focus: behavior, access, and policy options.
decisions: Target audience defined: public health newsletter
entities: Prepared/ready-to-eat/food delivery use (+30%): Likely longer-term (though may soften from peaks). Assumption: Expanded delivery infrastructure and persistent time pressures keep higher usage than pre-event levels. (speculative)
Plant-based product purchases (+40%): Likely longer-term. Assumption: Ongoing availability and sustained consumer interest in health and sustainability will continue to support demand. (speculative)

Frequency of home-cooked meals (-20%): Mixed/partly short-lived. Assumption: The pandemic cooking surge has partly rebounded while long-term convenience preferences have lowered the baseline rate. (speculative)
Online information and social media play a larger role in shaping food choices, while misinformation remains a challenge.
Rising food costs and budget constraints have led some adults to favor cheaper, calorie-dense options, widening disparities in diet quality.
Many pandemic-era habits, such as increased home cooking, partially reverted, but persistent convenience needs mean lower baseline cooking for some. (speculative)
Plant-based shelf-space doubled in the local grocery over recent seasons, indicating stronger local demand and retailer confidence in plant-based products.
Meal kits can substitute for takeout, reducing takeout frequency for some people.
Intermittent fasting trial: increased evening snacking observed (personal data point).
Plant proteins interest signal from a farmers' market demo: attendees asked more about plant proteins and how to use them than about meat alternatives.
Anecdotal evidence: a teenager brings their own lunch to school due to online food influencers, illustrating influencer impact on teen lunch choices.
TikTok and short-video platforms amplify visually appealing but less healthy recipes, potentially normalizing indulgent options and affecting perceived healthiness.
Late-night delivery availability (increasing coverage) potentially shifts dinner timing toward later in the evening and increases grazing or on-demand eating, including more single-person meals.
Snack subscription boxes introduce novelty packaged snacks into regular consumption, potentially increasing variety and frequency of snacking.
Skipped breakfast in workplace poll: nearly 50% skipped breakfast at least some days, indicating time pressures or changing schedules.
Anecdotal evidence: family changes soda habits after health scare, illustrating impact of health events on beverage choices and family norms around sugary drinks.
Saturday community garden serves as a popular small-scale food access point for younger residents.
Economist podcast claim: food price swings may prompt more home cooking.
open: Clarify region or locale (global, regional, country-specific)
Confirm whether citations or visuals are required and preferred format
Decision on including a campaign wording sidebar in future briefs
Assess evidence base for price-swing-driven increase in at-home cooking.
